intent: Minimal mpgd starter showing platform, target-config, i18n, game-services, asset, and simulation boundaries.

gotchas: Keep dense HUD or settings in DOM overlays for downstream games.
Do not make the scene the source of truth for progression.
Treat orientation locks as soft prompts unless the active platform adapter explicitly supports hard locking.
Do not store Phaser objects in simulation state.
Keep SDK imports out of starter scenes.
Use target-config as the source of feature availability.
Choose responsive, preferred, or locked orientation policy before adding resize behavior.
Treat platform callbacks as evidence, not grants.
Require backend ledger APIs for purchase, ad reward, and leaderboard records.
Fall back to a local guest and home intent when an older host does not implement additive methods.
Use the server-verified session player ID for backend services when it is available.
Keep the inline mode launch screen independent of Phaser and game runtime imports until the user starts gameplay.
Keep the expanded mode game document mapped to the game entrypoint in devvit.json.
Inline mode gameplay must use tap or click input without trapping feed scrolling.
Use the Devvit adapter web API instead of importing the Devvit SDK in game code.
Do not let raw file names become the gameplay API.
Register every runtime asset in the manifest.

manual: Target, player, effective config, and backend mode render on the starter screen.
Starter context resolves an identity session and launch intent without scene-level SDK imports.
Pointer or Enter starts the play scene.
The play scene updates from serializable simulation state.
Viewport orientation policy is visible and does not force unsafe WebView hard locks.
